mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 65% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.4 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth that drives corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the industrial production index reflect immediate supply chain bottlenecks and manufacturing slowdowns that trigger short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative reaction to INDPRO declines during periods of confirmed supply chain disruptions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a stable or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive monetary policy that erodes real corporate margins and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL rises above 0.3% alongside non-declining FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and supports long-term corporate revenue projections, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns exhibit a negative or zero correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% confirms economic recession conditions that force immediate equity deleveraging and generate negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 contracts by over 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial output and falling unemployment signals labor market tightness that constrains production capacity and triggers short-term negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative reaction when INDPRO rises concurrently with UNRATE declines over a 2-year sample.
